message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "3 alike runes form a block: 60 G base premium"
Passing tests so far: 6

Test sends 3 items of type "rune". Expects premium 71 (60 base + 6 first-insurance + 5 fee).

Without block logic, 3 runes would yield 3*25=75 base, +7.5 first insurance = 82.5, ceil + 5 = 88 (or different depending on rounding order).

The block rule (per spec): "A building block of 3 alike components is offered at a special base premium of 60 G." So when there are exactly 3 alike components of the same type, replace the base for those 3 with 60 (single block) instead of 75. The first-insurance surcharge then applies on the resulting base.

Minimal implementation: identify components (rune is a component; non-component types: sword/amulet/staff/potion). Group component items by type. For each group, count items. The base for that group:
- For groups of exactly 3 same type: 60
- Otherwise: 25 * count

Then sum group bases as the components subtotal. Each component is still ONE item (each gets its own first-insurance treatment? No — the first-insurance is per item but it scales with base premium of each item). 

Per spec it says "first insurance surcharge per item": each item gets 10% of its base. For a block, simplest interpretation: the block has total base 60, so the surcharge is 60 * 10% = 6. So treat the block as a single unit for surcharge. 60 + 6 = 66, +5 fee = 71. Matches.

Implement minimally to pass this test while keeping the previous 6 tests green. You'll need to differentiate components from main items, group component items by type, and apply block premiums for groups of exactly 3.

Existing main-item tests (sword/amulet/staff/potion) still need to work — each is one item, no grouping.

Existing single-rune test: one rune, group count 1, base 25, first insurance 2.5, sum 27.5, ceil + 5 = 33. ✓

Run `pnpm test`. Working dir: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-34-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking-2

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current state and implement minimal code to pass the failing test.
Now I'll implement the minimal code to handle the block of 3 alike runes.

content.text:
All 7 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added component grouping in `itemsSubtotal`. Components (currently just "rune") are collected by type; a group of exactly 3 alike components is priced as a single block (base 60), with first-insurance surcharge applied to the block as a unit. Non-component items and non-block-sized component groups keep the per-item pricing.
- Result: All 7 tests pass (29 still todo).
- Approach: Minimal — only handles the exactly-3-alike case demanded by the current test; other group sizes fall back to the existing per-item pricing already validated by the single-rune test. No future logic (no 4-rune behavior, no mixed-type blocks) was added.

Relevant file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-34-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking-2/src/claim-office.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
